items.title: 'Sad for us': Hannah Clarke's parents step back from advocacy
items.description: After their daughter and three grandchildren were murdered, Sue and Lloyd Clarke began a charity to improve awareness of coercive control. Now they've decided they need to step back for their own health.

items.title: What we know about Israel killing Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil
items.description: Khalil was killed and her colleague injured in what Lebanese officials are calling a 'double-tap' strike by Israel.

items.title: Another dinosaur discovery: Scientists discover ‘Bicharracosaurus’ in Patagonia
items.description: Historic discovery in Patagonia as scientists confirm remains of new dinosaur; CONICET researchers reveal key Jurassic species for understanding the evolution of sauropods in Patagonia. Leer más

items.title: EU unblocks €90 billion Ukraine loan after Hungary row
items.description: Diplomats said the definitive sign-off should come by Thursday (April 23, 2026) as Budapest waits for Russian oil to arrive through the Druzhba pipeline after Kyiv said operations had restarted

items.title: Dassault Systemes reports first-quarter revenue in line with estimates
Morocco’s Mohammed VI Tower rises over Rabat-Salé skyline
items.description: The Mohammed VI Tower, now the tallest building in Morocco, is reshaping the skyline of the Rabat-Salé region after its inauguration earlier in April 2026.

items.title: Nearly 8,000 migrants dead or missing in 2025
items.description: Nearly 8,000 people died or disappeared on migration routes in 2025, according to the United Nations, a stark reminder that for many, the journey in search of safety or opportunity remains deadly.

items.title: Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanese journalist; Tehran-US talks stalled
items.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon killed at least five people on Wednesday, including Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil.

items.title: Germany news: Bavarian police arrest suspected spies
items.description: Two men have been arrested in Bavaria and charged with espionage, Frankfurt Airport's new Terminal 3 has opened its doors, and former US President Obama has praised a German climate activist. DW has more.

items.title: US charges anti-extremism organisation over payments to informants in hate groups
items.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center said it would "vigorously defend ourselves, our staff, and our work".

items.title: Will another film star be able to sway the election in India’s Tamil Nadu?
items.description: Political commentators believe C Joseph Vijay can at best be a disruptor in what has become a three-cornered contest.

items.title: ‘Hellhole on the planet’: Trump reposts critique targeting India, China over birthright citizenship
items.description: Conservative author and radio host Michael Savage argued that such practices create a loophole where "a baby here becomes an instant citizen, and then they bring the entire family in from China or India or some other hellhole on the planet."

items.title: Avoid men, German police association chief tells women
items.description: Police association chief Dirk Peglow has stirred debate with his advice to women to avoid relationships with men for safety's sake. But his provocative tip is not without reason, as statistics show.

items.title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to stand trial at ICC over deadly ‘war on drugs’
items.description: The International Criminal Court on Thursday confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte, paving the way for a landmark trial. Duterte is accused of murder and attempted murder linked to his anti-drugs campaign, in a major test for the court amid mounting political pressure.

items.title: Islamic militants kill 11 people, burn homes in late-night attack in Nigeria
items.description: It was the latest violence in Africa's most populous country, which has long been battling a complex security crisis.

items.title: Zelensky to join EU leaders in Cyprus after 90-bn-euro loan unblocked
items.description: EU leaders will welcome Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday in Cyprus to celebrate the release of a 90-billion-euro ($105 billion) loan for Kyiv after a prolonged deadlock. Preliminary approval for the badly needed money came on Wednesday but a definitive sign-off is expected by Thursday, before European Union leaders hold talks with Zelensky in Cyprus's Ayia Napa marina. The funds were blocked after a bitter row broke out between Hungary's nationalist premier Viktor Orban and Zelensky.

items.title: New round of talks in Washington: Lebanon, Israel to discuss truce extension
items.description: Lebanese ​and Israeli envoys will meet for the second time in two weeks in Washington on Thursday, building on nascent contacts between the states, with Lebanon hoping for the extension of a shaky ceasefire between Hezbollah and Israel. The 10-day, US-mediated ceasefire is set to expire on Sunday. For more, Stuart Norval is joined by FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Philip Turle and Noga Tarnopolsky reporting from Jerusalem.

items.title: French Cup: Nice reach final after victory at Strasbourg
items.description: Nice secured a 2–0 win away at Strasbourg in the French Cup semi-final and will face Lens in the final. Elye Wahi made the difference with a brace. Initially dominated by a Strasbourg side sitting eighth in Ligue 1, Nice, 15th, remained defensively solid and clinically efficient in attack. They will return to the Stade de France on May 22, four years after losing their previous final to Nantes (1–0).

items.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
items.description: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

items.title: Russia and Libya vow to strengthen bilateral cooperation
items.description: Russia is ready to assist Libya in “restoring unity and national reconciliation,” Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Tuesday after holding talks with Libya’s acting foreign minister, Taher Al-Baour, in Moscow.

items.title: World Press Photo announces Photo of the Year 2026
items.description: The executive director of World Press Photo said this image shows the inconsolable grief of children losing their father in a place built for justice. It is a stark and necessary record of family separation following the U.S. reform policies.

items.title: Amnesty International paints a grim picture for human rights
items.description: In its 2025/2026 report, the human rights watchdog Amnesty International calls out the "predatory behavior of the powerful" and calls for action to defend the global order.

items.title: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says
items.description: Reports say Phelan, whose role was largely administrative, left amid tension over US shipbuilding.

items.title: Iran targets three ships in Strait of Hormuz escalation
items.description: A global shipping route is once again under threat as Iranian forces move against commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Pentagon removes top Navy official amid military shake-up
items.description: US Secretary of the Navy John Phelan has left his post with immediate effect, the Pentagon said Wednesday, without providing an explanation. His departure comes amid a broader clear-out of senior military figures under President Donald Trump, as the United States remains locked in an unresolved conflict with Iran.

items.title: Canberrans with disability fear NDIS changes will cut vital support
items.description: Canberrans living with disability warn planned changes to the NDIS could strip back support, undermining their mental health and sense of certainty.

items.title: Lebanon to seek ceasefire extension in U.S.-hosted talks with Israel
items.description: The U.S.-mediated ceasefire has yielded a significant reduction in violence, but attacks have continued in southern Lebanon, where Israeli troops have seized a self-declared buffer zone

items.title: Niger junta says it suspended nearly 3,000 NGOs in 2025
items.description: Niger's military government suspended the activities of 2,900 of the 4,700 registered local and foreign non-governmental organisations and development agencies, the interior minister said.

items.title: Ukraine says some Russian missiles fly near Chornobyl, risking major accident
items.description: Prosecutor General Ruslan Kravchenko detailed the previously unreported Russian military activity near Ukrainian nuclear sites in written ​remarks, as Ukraine prepares to mark on April 26, 40th anniversary of the 1986 Chornobyl disaster

items.title: Trust trumps everything else: Anthropic’s India chief Irina Ghose
items.description: At the AWS Summit in Bengaluru, Irina Ghose, Managing Director of India at Anthropic, highlighted the importance of customers’ trust and reliable products that serve all users

items.title: US considers sending 1,100 Afghans to DR Congo amid resettlement halt
items.description: The Trump administration is considering a controversial new plan, to send more than 1,000 Afghans who supported US forces, not to America, but to central Africa.

items.title: Israeli forces levelling Lebanese villages behind ‘yellow line’
items.description: Israeli forces levelling Lebanese villages behind ‘yellow line’

items.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 55 after Trump extended ceasefire?
items.description: Iran says it wants talks, but blames US “breach of commitments, blockade and threats” for stalling negotiations.

items.title: Israel-Iran LIVE: Pakistan Minister, U.S. envoy discuss efforts for 2nd round of U.S.-Iran talks
items.description: Iran’s vow not to reopen the Strait of Hormuz so long as a U.S. naval blockade remained in place

items.title: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
items.description: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

items.title: Billionaire's ex-staffer denied bail over $1.6m fraud allegations
items.description: Billionaire Judith Neilson's former secretary is accused of going on a $1.6m shopping spree using her employer's credit cards and bank information.

items.title: Four dead in Russia, Ukraine after exchange of drone strikes
items.description: As diplomatic talks have stalled and neither side has made a breakthrough on the front, Moscow and Kyiv have both stepped up their long-range drone attacks, firing swarms of the unmanned explosive-packed devices at each other on a nightly basis

items.title: Iran releases video of IRGC seizing ship in the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Iran’s revolutionary guard has released a video said to show its forces seizing a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Second French peacekeeper dies in Lebanon after ambush blamed on Hezbollah
items.description: A second French soldier died on Wednesday from wounds suffered in a weekend ambush against UN peacekeepers in Lebanon blamed on Hezbollah, President Emmanuel Macron said. A first soldier, staff sergeant Florian Montorio, was shot dead in the Saturday ambush, for which Hezbollah has denied responsibility.

items.title: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
items.description: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

items.title: As millions return to Sudan, UN calls for investment in essential services
items.description: Three years into the devastating conflict in Sudan, nearly four million displaced people have returned to their places of origin across the country, only to face “another struggle for survival”, the UN International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Tuesday.

items.title: Iran says won't reopen Hormuz if U.S. upholds naval blockade
items.description: While strikes around the region have mostly ceased since the truce began, there has been no letup in the pressure around the crucial trade route, with the two sides exercising their economic leverage in a bid to force the other to back down.

items.title: BBC visits migrant camp in northern France as new deal announced
items.description: A proposed three-year deal with Paris is aimed at stopping migrants crossing the Channel on small boats.

items.title: Girl, 14, found in singer D4vd's car died of 'multiple injuries'
items.description: The medical examiner said the 14-year-old's cause of death was determined months ago but was blocked from release.

items.title: Rodrigo Duterte to face ICC trial after judges confirm charges
items.description: Pre-trial judges “unanimously confirmed all the charges... against Rodrigo Roa Duterte and committed him to trial,” the ICC said in a statement

items.title: Iran war: Standoff at Hormuz casts shadow over Iran ceasefire talks
items.description: The prospects for renewed peace talks wavered after Iran seized two international vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. DW has the latest.

items.title: Germany's finance minister wants to scrap spousal tax splitting
items.description: Highly controversial, but not well understood: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil wants to change the tax benefits for married couples.

items.title: A booklover's dream holiday in Scotland
items.description: As UNESCO celebrates World Book Day on April 23, we visit a Scottish coastal town that was saved by books.

items.title: Amnesty International Argentina warns Milei government scaling back protections
items.description: Report by NGO’s local chapter says government’s cuts have deepened inequality, with protests, the press and gender violence and abortion protections under increasing pressure. Leer más

items.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
items.description: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

items.title: French teenager Seixas becomes youngest Fleche Wallonne winner
items.description: French prodigy Paul Seixas at 19 became the youngest winner of the La Fleche Wallonne one-day classic on Wednesday in his first ride in the race. Seixas beat home Switzerland's Mauro Schmid and British rider Ben Tulett to replace Philemon De Meersman in the race's history books as the Belgian was 21 years old and 150 days when he won in 1936.

items.title: How the new eligibility test for NDIS participants could work
items.description: Labor's plan to create a new "assessment tool" to determine future NDIS eligibility based on functional need could backfire without the right settings, say experts.

items.title: Israeli strike kills five in Gaza, including three children
items.description: Israel has committed 2,400 violations of the 'ceasefire' deal struck with Hamas in October, Gaza officials say.

items.title: India news: Tamil Nadu, West Bengal cast votes
items.description: Polling has begun in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal in high-stakes assembly elections. DW has more.

items.title: Award-winning author who put Brisbane on the literary map dies aged 92
items.description: Erudite and urbane, David Malouf — perhaps most famous for his portrait of mid-century Brisbane in his 1975 novel Johnno — will be fondly remembered for his immeasurable contribution to Australian letters.

items.title: Daniel Noboa’s ‘new Ecuador’ has some fearing loss of civil freedoms
items.description: Ecuador's Daniel Noboa appears to be consolidating power in ways that are causing growing unease in civil society. One week it’s political parties, the next it’s the courts. Now it’s the media. Leer más

items.title: Defendants plead not guilty over 2025 Nigeria coup plot
items.description: Six men pleaded not guilty to treason and terrorism charges over a botched coup plot, Nigeria media reported Wednesday, as the first prosecutions for the alleged 2025 putsch attempt get under way.

items.title: YPF investors say they’ll arbitrate $16-billion Argentina case
items.description: Former YPF investors who saw US$16.1-billion US judgment against Argentina thrown out on appeal last month tell trial judge that they intend to pursue international treaty arbitration and further appeals. Leer más

items.title: Pakistan Minister, U.S. envoy discuss efforts for 2nd round of U.S.-Iran talks
items.description: The meeting comes amid stepped-up diplomatic engagements aimed at bringing Tehran and Washington to the negotiating table, possibly by the weekend

items.title: Nigeria's Tinubu sacks finance minister in surprise cabinet reshuffle
items.description: Nigeria's President Bola Tinubu has sacked his finance minister Wale Edun as part of a minor cabinet reshuffle. Stepping into the role is former Minister of State for Finance Taiwo Oyedele. He played a central role in the administration's tax overhaul which took effect at the start of this year.

items.title: Putin's approval rating falls amid state-imposed internet restrictions
items.description: In Russia, expanding state restrictions on the Internet are frustrating daily life for millions — and sending President Putin's approval ratings on a rare downward spiral.

items.title: After being held up for months, Ukraine set to get $100B loan from EU
items.description: Taking advantage of the electoral defeat of Hungary's Viktor Orban, European Union countries have moved to approve a $100 billion loan to Ukraine.

items.title: Racing to green the world's data centers
items.description: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

items.title: South Sudan to hold elections in December as fighting rages on
items.description: After years of delays, South Sudan is expected to hold elections in December, despite the nation being on the brink of civil war.

items.title: Israel kills journalist and wounds another in south Lebanon targeted attack
items.description: Israel has killed journalist Amal Khalil and injured Zeinab Faraj in a ‘double-tap’ attack in southern Lebanon.

items.title: Zambia's government takes possession of ex-president's body in repatriation row
items.description: Zambia authorities and Edgar Lungu's family are in a dispute over where former leader's remains should be buried.

items.title: US accuses China of 'intimidation campaign' to block Taiwan president’s Africa trip
items.description: The United States on Wednesday accused China of pressuring African nations to block a planned visit by Taiwan’s President Lai Ching-te, after several countries revoked overflight permits. Beijing denied the claims while expressing "high appreciation" for the states in question as tensions over Taiwan’s international engagement continue to heighten.

items.title: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
items.description: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

items.title: Prediction market Kalshi docks three US candidates for betting on own races
items.description: Penalties come amid calls for greater oversight; company pledges to proactively police 'insider trading' on platforms.

items.title: AI boom lifts Nokia sales, shares hit 16-year high after earnings beat
New grapevine virus GINV detected for first time in Australia
items.description: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

items.title: Police officer kept job after allegedly assaulting boy, stalking woman
items.description: A NSW police officer accused of throwing a two-year-old boy "headfirst at the ground" — as well as stalking and assaulting a woman — kept his job after several unsuccessful prosecutions.

items.title: Two Pakistanis to be China’s first foreign astronauts: reports
items.description: A Pakistani astronaut in orbit ahead of an Indian one as part of the ‘Gaganyaan’ mission is at minimum a reversal of the usual regional optics

items.title: Canavan's frosty foray into Farrer's campaign trail
items.description: This week the Albanese government pushed out a major reform ahead of the federal budget, the Nationals leader was out in the cold on the campaign trail and a former Treasury secretary had some choice words about the push for gas tax reform.

items.title: Yamal scores but injured as Barcelona beat Celta to restore La Liga lead
items.description: Barcelona edge closer to sealing their La Liga title defence with win against Champions League-chasing Celta Vigo.

items.title: More than 50% of children in Argentina are poor, says UCA report
items.description: According to a report by the Observatory of Social Debt at the Catholic University of Argentina (UCA), poverty rate among children stood at 53.6% at the end of 2025. Leer más

items.title: UN leadership hopefuls stress need for peace and restoring confidence
items.description: Contenders for next UN secretary-general made their case this week for a United Nations more invested in peace, but avoided taking positions that could antagonize the member states who will choose the world body's next chief. Chile's Michelle Bachelet, Argentina's Rafael Grossi, Costa Rica's Rebeca Grynspan and Senegal's Macky Sall are all hoping to succeed Antonio Guterres on January 1, 2027, when his second five-year term ends. Each candidate spent three hours this week answering wide-ranging questions from the 193 member states and representatives of civil society.

items.title: India to Iran: How two wars shaped the rise of Pakistan’s Asim Munir
items.description: The war against India a year ago led to Munir's elevation to the status of field marshal. Now he's playing peacemaker.

items.title: School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes
items.description: Iran's schools are closed because of the war. Classes are being held via TV programs and the national intranet. Children without a laptop or smartphone are left out. The country is effectively cut off from the internet.

items.title: Iran tightens control of Hormuz after US calls off renewed attacks
items.description: Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as it tightened its grip on ​the strategic waterway after US President Donald Trump announced he was indefinitely calling off attacks, with no sign of peace talks restarting. The status of a two-week-old ceasefire, due to have expired earlier this week, remained unclear.

items.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as fuel prices surge
items.description: The airline is the latest to cut flights as the US-Israel war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring.

items.title: How TikTok is driving American expats to Southeast Asia
items.description: Americans who moved to Vietnam and Thailand say their lives are now lower-stress and lower-cost. But glamorous videos on TikTok don't tell the whole story.

items.title: Exclusive-SpaceX IPO filing shows Elon Musk can retain board control
Chihuahua operation: Mexico, US trade harsh words over deaths of two US agents
items.description: Mexico's government and the White House sparred Wednesday over the death of two Americans in connection to an anti-drug raid, after US media reported the officials were CIA personnel. The two Americans died in a car accident on Sunday in the northern state of Chihuahua. Two Mexican investigators also died in the crash.

items.title: Virginia approves redistricting measure, boosting Democrats' hopes for midterms
items.description: The new maps approved by the state's voters could allow Democrats to flip as many as four House seats.

items.title: China's Xpeng expects to start delivering 'flying' cars in 2027
items.description: The Chinese electric vehicle maker has received more than 7,000 orders for its flying cars, the majority of which are in China.

items.title: Samsung workers rally in South Korea, demanding higher pay, threatening to strike
items.description: Samsung's union, which represents about 74,000 workers, says the company has failed to offer adequate compensation despite its strong performance

items.title: Analysis:Investors return to US stocks as AI, earnings growth feed fear of missing out
Live: Wounded Bulldogs up for the fight against league-leading Swans
items.description: The injury-hit Western Bulldogs face Sydney after St Kilda successfully reduces forward Lance Collard's ban for using a homophobic slur to four weeks with two suspended. Follow live.

items.title: Gaza’s second front: The battle against disease-carrying rats
items.description: Palestinian families in Gaza endure daily terror from rats in unsanitary camps, but help is hard to come by.

items.title: Lamine Yamal’s World Cup status unclear after Barcelona injury
items.description: Lamine Yamal pulled up injured when scoring the winning goal for Barcelona in their La Liga win against Celta Vigo.

items.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'
items.description: With Bundesliga titles in the bag and cup finals to come, Bayern's men and women have a chance to achieve something unique in Europe. The two teams dominate in Germany and appear united in their Champions League aims.

items.title: Israeli strikes kill 3 in Lebanon, Beirut to seek truce extension
items.description: Ahead of the talks on Thursday (April 23, 2026), Israel called on the Lebanese government to "work together" with it against Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah

items.title: South Korea says security talks with U.S. should not be linked to Coupang probe
items.description: The Ministry commented in response to a media report that Washington has ‌threatened to halt security talks unless Seoul provided legal guarantees for the safety of Coupang’s Chairman Kim Bom during a probe into the leaking ‌of customer information at the company

items.title: Russia's Vladimir Putin hosts Seychelles President Patrick Herminie at Kremlin
items.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met Seychelles President Patrick Herminie at the Kremlin on Wednesday for talks focused on bilateral ties and international issues.

items.title: Samsung workers protest over huge pay gap with SK Hynix, threaten long strike
Pope Leo criticises Equatorial Guinea prisons as he winds up Africa tour
items.description: The Pope gives a frank and passionate speech, the likes of which have characterised his tour of Africa.

items.title: 'You are not alone': Pope hits out at jails in closed-off Equatorial Guinea
items.description: Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday made a tightly controlled visit to a notorious prison in Equatorial Guinea's biggest city, after launching a rare criticism of living conditions for inmates. Prisoners lined up in the freshly repainted courtyard of Bata prison, breaking into song and dance in the driving rain as they greeted the leader of the world's 1.4 billion Catholics.

items.title: Nepal: Interior minister elected on anti-corruption platform steps down amid financial investigation
items.description: Nepal's new government elected on an anti-corruption platform after youth protests toppled its predecessor has now lost two ministers in weeks. Sudan Gurung said he was quitting to ensure "a fair investigation."

items.title: Lebanon says Israel targeted journalist killed in airstrike
items.description: Amal Khalil was killed when a house she was sheltering in was hit by an Israeli airstrike. Lebanon has accused Israel of targeting media workers as part of an "established approach" — a charge Israel denies.

items.title: China condemns Japanese leaders' war shrine offerings, visits
items.description: Japan’s “negative ‌actions” concerning ‌the ⁠shrine “grossly trample on ⁠human conscience”, and China is “very indignant at these ​heinous ‌acts and strongly condemns them”, says a spokesperson for Beijing’s ‌Foreign Ministry

items.title: 'You are not alone': Pope Leo XIV delivers message hope to prisoners in Equatorial Guinea
items.description: Pope Leo XIV told inmates at one of Equatorial Guinea’s notorious prisons that they are not alone, as he drew attention Wednesday to prison conditions, human rights abuses and injustices that campaigners have denounced for years.

items.title: Japan: South Korean arrested outside Yasukuni Shrine ceremony for war dead
items.description: Police said a South Korean national holding a banner with political messages was arrested at Tokyo's Yasukuni Shrine to Japan's war dead. Japan's prime minister did not attend proceedings, but around 120 lawmakers did.

items.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
items.description: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people critically injured, authorities have said.

items.title: Exclusive-SpaceX conquered the stars, now eyes bigger opportunity in AI
Is taxing the super rich really viable in Latin America?
items.description: New report proposing minimal levy on individuals with huge asset wealth in Latin America proposes a way to tax extreme wealth, but the idea also faces resistance. Leer más

items.title: Pope Leo visits prison in Equatorial Guinea
items.description: Pope Leo XIV visited a prison in Equatorial Guinea on the last day of his Africa tour.

items.title: Middle East war live: Lebanon meets Israel in Washington to request truce extension
items.description: Israel and Lebanon hold a new round of talks in Washington on Thursday, during which Beirut plans to request a one-month extension of a ceasefire due to expire within days. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Nepal Home Minister Sudhan Gurung resigns amid questions over financial conduct
Palantir: Why its political manifesto is causing a stir
items.description: The US tech company has posted a 22-point manifesto on X that goes well beyond typical corporate communications. Critics say it is evidence of "technofascism."

items.title: Djibouti's Guelleh reelected for sixth presidential term: official results
items.description: Ismail Omar Guelleh has won a sixth term as President of Djibouti, the country's constitutional council has confirmed. Guelleh won 97.01% of the vote, the council said on Tuesday.

items.title: Reza Pahlavi calls for regime change in Iran on Berlin visit
items.description: The former Shah's son is in Germany to drum up support for his global campaign to overthrow Iran's leadership. During a speech in the capital, he described the German government's refusal to meet with him "a disgrace."

items.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
items.description: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

items.title: Can fish hook voters in India’s West Bengal elections?
items.description: In battle for India's West Bengal election, PM Modi's men campaigned with fish in hand to try to connect with voters.

items.title: Iran-Israel war updates: Iran says won't reopen Hormuz as long as U.S. blockade remains
items.description: Iran Guards say 'seized' two ships attempting to cross Strait of Hormuz; Iran says it has yet to decide whether to join a new round of talks with the U.S.

items.title: Honeywell quarterly sales rise on higher pricing and demand
Fight over legal costs from Gina Rinehart judgement begins
items.description: A legal wrangle is underway over what costs mining heiress Gina Rinehart is liable for following last week's judgement in her epic court battle with the family of her father's former business partner.

items.title: The battle of the blockades in the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: As part of its blockade of Iran, ships have been seized by US forces, others fired upon by Iran and, most recently, boarded by the regime's revolutionary guards. This is all while both sides maintain there is a ceasefire.

items.title: STMicroelectronics' beats on results and guidance boost shares
The rise and fall of East Germany's Palace of the Republic
items.description: From 1976 through 1990, the building in East Berlin served as a symbol of the communist state's power. The "People's Palace" was controversially destroyed — and yet remains present.

items.title: INDEC: Economic activity in Argentina fell 2.6% in February
items.description: Economic activity posts steepest drop since December 2023, INDEC says; Manufacturing and commerce lead declines in February, gains for mining and agriculture. Leer más

items.title: South Korean court cuts jail time for battery CEO over deadly plant fire
items.description: A court sentenced Aricell CEO Park Soon-kwan to 15 years in prison in September last year, saying the company's practices had prioritised profit over worker safety

items.title: Top ministers quit after Peru’s president postpones F-16 fighter jet deal
items.description: The Trump administration has warned against Peru pulling out of the deal, saying it reflects 'bad faith' negotiations.

items.title: Albanese poised to kill off move to increase taxes on gas giants
items.description: The government will not increase taxes on gas giants in next month's budget, echoing the industry's argument that exporters are already paying tens of billions of dollars in taxes each year.

items.title: Fifty days out, who is guaranteed to make Argentina’s World Cup squad?
items.description: Coach Lionel Scaloni has the majority of his squad for the tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico decided – a look at the problematic positions and how many spots are up for grabs. Leer más

items.title: Tomb of Pope Francis draws thousands of faithful in year since his death
items.description: One year after the death of Pope Francis, thousands of faithful still line up every day to pass by his tomb.

items.title: Daylesford intersection where five people died to become green space
items.description: An intersection in a country Victorian town where five people died will be closed after $1 million in funding was offered as part of the Black Spots Program.

items.title: Zelensky to join European leaders in Cyprus as EU unblocks €90bn Ukraine loan
items.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky is scheduled to meet with EU leaders Thursday in Cyprus as the bloc prepares to approve a €90 billion loan for Ukraine, ending a prolonged deadlock. Hungary premier Viktor Orban had vetoed the funds until Kyiv repaired a pipeline hit by a Russian strike – work now completed, allowing oil flows to resume – shortly after his electoral defeat to pro-EU rival Peter Magyar.

items.title: Milei’s auction of Argentine railroads risks sidelining US-backed bidder
items.description: The model Milei's administration has chosen for the auctioning off of Argentina’s decaying freight railways has drawn criticism from industry experts and risks sidelining one of the most prominent bidders. Leer más

items.title: How grievance turns to violence in a mass shooter's mind
items.description: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

items.title: South Africa could see first winter without power cuts in years
items.description: In South Africa, the state-owned electricity company Eskom announced on Wednesday that it does not expect power outages during the winter months in the Southern Hemisphere, from April to August. This will be the first winter without power cuts since the company has been facing difficulties.

She offers analysis on the growing geopolitical instability surrounding Iran, energy security, and the EU’s strategic position in an increasingly fragmented global order. According to Ms. Neumann, the central issue is not merely the volatility of negotiations, but their lack of clarity and coherence. From her vantage point, "the main problem is that they don't even have a clear focus on what they are negotiating about".

items.title: Watch: Donald Trump should lift the blockade | The Hindu Editorial

What West Asia needs is economic and strategic stability, not forever wars.

items.title: 'Impossible Job' of UN Secretary-General: Presiding over 193 member states with competing interests
items.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Dr. Alexandra Novosseloff, Associate Research Fellow at the Thucydides Centre of Paris II Panthéon-Assas University. According to Dr. Novosseloff, the UN is a living reflection of the geopolitical tensions that shape our world. In assessing the current race for Secretary-General, she focuses less on individual personalities and more on the structural constraints that define the role itself. The Secretary-General operates within a system driven by competing state interests, where leadership is less about authority and more about navigation.

items.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill Lebanese journalist, wound another
items.description: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon on Wednesday killed Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil and wounded a photographer accompanying her, according to a senior Lebanese military official and Khalil's employer, the Al-Akhbar newspaper. The death of Khalil, 43, brought the death toll on Wednesday to five people. It was the deadliest day since a 10-day ceasefire was announced on April ‌16 to halt hostilities between Israel and Lebanese armed group Hezbollah.

items.title: Train collision in Denmark prompts massive emergency response near Copenhagen

The accident prompted a massive emergency response, and several injured passengers were flown to a hospital, local authorities said.

items.title: In Algerian desert, Sahrawi refugees still dream of independent homeland
items.description: Sahrawi refugees go about daily life in the Aousserd camp near Tindouf in southern Algeria as residents say decades in exile have not weakened their demand for independence in Western Sahara: "Our demands are simple -- independence, and nothing else."

items.title: Pope in Equatorial Guinea: Moral authority role risks being 'whitewashed & used to legitimise power'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Tutu Alicante, international human rights lawyer.and anti-corruption activist. According to Mr. Alicante, the Pope's visit to Equatorial Guinea presents a paradox: it offers a rare opportunity to spotlight injustice, yet risks being appropriated by the regime for its own validation. Could the "global moral authority" inadvertently lend credence to the authoritarian regime? Equatorial Guinea's resources have failed to lift up society and translate into human dignity. Governance remains defined by exclusion, repression, and the erosion of accountability.
